metadata.title: The Abbreviated 510(k) Program
metadata.note: File name '17-505b2-applications.pdf' does not match document content. Actual document is 'The Abbreviated 510(k) Program — Guidance for Industry and Food and Drug Administration Staff' (September 13, 2019, CDRH/CBER). Rules extracted from actual content.

rules.rule_text: Any person required to register with FDA who proposes to introduce a device into interstate commerce for commercial distribution must submit a 510(k) to FDA at least 90 days before commercial distribution begins.
rules.verbatim_quote: Under section 510(k) of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FD&C Act), each person required to register with FDA and who proposes to introduce a device into interstate commerce for commercial distribution is required to submit a 510(k) to FDA at least 90 days before commercial distribution is to begin.

rules.rule_text: The Abbreviated 510(k) Program does not alter any statutory or regulatory requirements related to the premarket notification process under sections 510 and 513 of the FD&C Act and 21 CFR 807 Subpart E.
rules.verbatim_quote: The Abbreviated 510(k) Program does not alter any statutory or regulatory requirements related to the premarket notification process under sections 510 and 513 of the FD&C Act, and 21 CFR 807 subpart E.

rules.rule_text: A manufacturer may choose to submit an Abbreviated 510(k) when the submission relies on one or more of: FDA guidance document(s); demonstration of compliance with special controls; and/or voluntary consensus standard(s).
rules.verbatim_quote: Therefore, device manufacturers may choose to submit an Abbreviated 510(k) when the submission relies on one or more: FDA guidance document(s); Demonstration of compliance with special controls for the device type, either in a device-specific classification regulation or a special controls guidance document; and/or Voluntary consensus standard(s).

rules.rule_text: An Abbreviated 510(k) submission must include all required information identified in 21 CFR 807.87, including device description, intended use, indications for use, and proposed labeling.
rules.verbatim_quote: An Abbreviated 510(k) submission must include the required information identified in 21 CFR 807.87.

rules.rule_text: For an Abbreviated 510(k) relying on an FDA guidance document and/or special control(s), the submission must include a summary report describing how the guidance(s) were used to demonstrate substantial equivalence and/or how the device complies with the special control(s).
rules.verbatim_quote: manufacturers submitting an Abbreviated 510(k) that relies on an FDA guidance document and/or special control(s) should include a summary report that describes how the guidance document(s) were used to demonstrate substantial equivalence and/or how the device complies with special control(s).

rules.rule_text: The summary report must include information regarding any deviations (alternative methods used to demonstrate substantial equivalence not described in the guidance document).
rules.verbatim_quote: If recommendations in guidance document(s) are used to support substantial equivalence, the summary report should include information regarding any deviations, that is any alternative methods used to demonstrate substantial equivalence that are not described in the guidance.

rules.rule_text: For an Abbreviated 510(k) relying on voluntary consensus standard(s), the submission must include the information described in Appendix A and a declaration of conformity.
rules.verbatim_quote: Manufacturers submitting an Abbreviated 510(k) that relies on a voluntary consensus standard(s) should provide the information described in Appendix A and a declaration of conformity as recommended by the guidance document 'Appropriate Use of Voluntary Consensus Standards in Premarket Submissions for Medical Devices.'
cross_references: FDA Guidance: Appropriate Use of Voluntary Consensus Standards in Premarket Submissions for Medical Devices

rules.rule_text: For an Abbreviated 510(k) relying on general use of a voluntary consensus standard, the submission must include the basis of such use along with the underlying information or data that supports how the standard was used.
rules.verbatim_quote: Manufacturers submitting an Abbreviated 510(k) that relies on general use of a voluntary consensus standard should include the basis of such use along with the underlying information or data that supports how the standard was used.

rules.rule_text: FDA intends to review Abbreviated 510(k) submissions within FDA's 90-day statutory deadline under section 510(n)(1) of the FD&C Act and MDUFA performance goals for 510(k) submissions.
rules.verbatim_quote: FDA intends to review Abbreviated 510(k) submissions within FDA's 90-day statutory deadline under section 510(n)(1) of the FD&C Act and Medical Device User Fee Amendments (MDUFA) performance goals for 510(k) submissions.
rules.exceptions: Subject to FDA's acceptance review in accordance with the Refuse to Accept Policy for 510(k)s.

rules.rule_text: If FDA does not believe an Abbreviated 510(k) submission is appropriate for review under the Abbreviated 510(k) Program, FDA intends to convert the submission to a Traditional 510(k) and notify the submitter.
rules.verbatim_quote: If a manufacturer submits an Abbreviated 510(k) that FDA does not believe is appropriate for review under the Abbreviated 510(k) Program, FDA intends to convert the submission to a Traditional 510(k) and notify the submitter.

rules.rule_text: An Abbreviated 510(k) must include a coversheet that clearly identifies the submission as an 'Abbreviated 510(k)'.
rules.verbatim_quote: A coversheet clearly identifying the submission as an 'Abbreviated 510(k)';

rules.rule_text: An Abbreviated 510(k) must include all information required under 21 CFR 807.87, including a description of the device, the intended use and the indications for use of the device, and the proposed labeling for the device.
rules.verbatim_quote: Information required under 21 CFR 807.87, including a description of the device, the intended use and the indications for use of the device, and the proposed labeling for the device.

rules.rule_text: For submissions relying on FDA guidance or special controls, the Abbreviated 510(k) summary report must summarize: device description, manufacturer's device design requirements, risk management information, and a description of test methods used to address performance characteristics.
rules.verbatim_quote: These reports summarize the device description, the manufacturer's device design requirements, risk management information, and a description of test methods used to address performance characteristics.

rules.rule_text: If a manufacturer chooses to use an alternative approach to address a particular issue, sufficient detail must be provided to justify that approach.
rules.verbatim_quote: If a manufacturer chooses to use an alternative approach to address a particular issue, sufficient detail should be provided to justify that approach;

rules.rule_text: An Abbreviated 510(k) must include data/information to address issues not covered by guidance documents, special controls, and/or voluntary consensus standards.
rules.verbatim_quote: Data/information to address issues not covered by guidance documents, special controls, and/or voluntary consensus standards;

rules.rule_text: An Abbreviated 510(k) must include the Indications for Use form (FDA Form 3881).

Special controls are defined as those controls, such as performance standards, postmarket surveillance, patient registries, development and dissemination of guidelines, recommendations, and other appropriate actions that provide reasonable assurance of the device's safety and effectiveness for Class II devices.
rules.verbatim_quote: Special controls are defined in section 513(a)(1)(B) of the FD&C Act as those controls, such as performance standards, postmarket surveillance, patient registries, development and dissemination of guidelines, recommendations and other appropriate actions that provide reasonable assurance of the device's safety and effectiveness.
cross_references: Public Law 101-629 (Safe Medical Devices Act of 1990)

rules.rule_text: When relying on an FDA-recognized voluntary consensus standard, manufacturers must consult the FDA Recognized Consensus Standards Database for the current edition of the standard.
rules.verbatim_quote: For the current edition of the FDA-recognized standard(s), see the FDA Recognized Consensus Standards Database.
